title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 压力性损伤的定义与好发部位：压力性损伤是由皮肤持续受压引起的伤口，也称为压疮、褥疮等，常发生在骨骼靠近皮肤的部位，如脚跟、踝部、髋部、尾骨和肘部。
text_en: A pressure injury or ulcer is a wound caused by constant pressure on your skin. It may also be called a pressure sore, pressure ulcer, bed sore, or de-cubitus ulcer. Pressure injuries occur most frequently in areas where your bones are close to the skin, including your heels, ankles, hips, tailbone, or elbows.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 皮肤的三层结构：表皮层（保护身体）、真皮层（供给营养、调节体温、提供弹性）和皮下组织层（储存脂肪、缓冲肌肉和骨骼）。
text_en: In order to understand how pressure injuries happen, it's important to know about the layers of the skin. Your skin is made up of three main layers. The top layer called the epidermis is a thin layer that protects your body from the environment. The thicker middle layer is called the dermis. Its main functions are to supply the epidermis with nutrients, help regulate your body temperature, and provide strength and elasticity to your skin. The innermost layer is called the hypodermis, or subcutaneous layer. This layer stores fat, which cushions the muscles and bones beneath it.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 压力性损伤的形成机制与分期概念：持续压力压迫血管，导致皮肤组织缺血缺氧坏死，形成压力性损伤；医疗人员根据组织损伤深度进行分期。
text_en: In a pressure injury, constant pressure on the skin over a long period of time can compress the blood vessels that supply the layers with the oxygen and nutrients they need to survive. If there's not enough blood flow, your skin tissue begins to die and a pressure injury If you have a pressure injury, your healthcare provider will classify it based on what can be known about the depth of tissue damage.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 1期和2期压力性损伤：1期表现为皮肤完整但发红、不可按压变白（非苍白性红斑）；2期表现为水疱或开放性溃疡，表皮和真皮部分缺失，周围皮肤发红。
text_en: This is called staging. In a stage 1 pressure injury, the skin is intact, red, and inflamed. The redness is non-blanchable. This means it doesn't become pale if you press on it. A stage 2 pressure injury appears as a blister or an open sore. There is partial thickness loss of the epidermis and dermis. The areas surrounding the sore may be red and irritated.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 3期和4期压力性损伤：3期为火山口样全层皮肤缺失，深达皮下脂肪层；4期最严重，全层组织缺失，深达肌肉或骨骼。
text_en: A stage 3 pressure injury is a crater-like, full thickness loss of skin. It extends down to the fat tissue of the hypodermis. Stage 4 is the most severe. A stage 4 pressure injury is a full thickness loss of tissue. It extends through all three layers of skin and into the underlying muscle or bone.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 不可分期压力性损伤与疑似深部组织损伤：不可分期损伤因被坏死组织覆盖而无法判断深度；疑似深部组织损伤表现为紫红色皮肤区域或含血性水疱，提示皮下软组织损伤。
text_en: An unstageable pressure injury is a full thickness loss of tissue. It's called unstageable because the injury is covered by dead tissue, making it hard to tell how deep it is. And a suspected deep tissue injury appears as a maroon or purple area of the skin. It may contain a blister filled with blood. It looks like this because of damaged soft tissue underneath your skin.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 压力性损伤的治疗方法：用清水、温和肥皂或生理盐水清洁伤口预防感染；严重时进行清创术去除坏死组织；使用专用敷料保护伤口并保持湿润促进愈合。
text_en: To prevent infection in your pressure injury, your doctor will clean your wound using water and a mild soap or saline solution. If your wound is severe, your doctor will debris or surgically remove the dead tissue from the pressure injury. To protect your wound and keep it from drying out while it heals, your doctor may cover it with a specialized bandage.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 香烟的成分：烟草叶含尼古丁及多种化合物，燃烧释放数千种有害化学物质（包括40余种致癌物）、一氧化碳、氮氧化物及放射性颗粒；所有烟草形式（雪茄、烟斗、无烟烟草）均有害
text_en: stream, where they spread to every organ in your body. A cigarette is made using the tobacco leaf, which contains nicotine and a variety of other compounds. As the tobacco and compounds burn, they release thousands of dangerous chemicals, including over 40 known to cause cancer. Cigarette smoke contains the poisonous gases carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide, as well as trace amounts of cancer-causing radioactive particles. All forms of tobacco are dangerous, including cigars, pipes, and smokeless tobacco, such as chewing tobacco and snuff.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 尼古丁的成瘾机制：尼古丁经血流到达大脑，诱发愉悦感；反复暴露导致大脑脱敏，需要更多尼古丁才能维持正常感觉
text_en: Nicotine is an addictive chemical in tobacco. After you inhale tobacco smoke, nicotine flows through the bloodstream to your brain, where it induces a pleasurable feeling. When you repeatedly expose your brain to nicotine, it becomes desensitized, making you crave more and more nicotine just to feel normal.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟导致的死亡与主要健康风险概述：吸烟者寿命缩短，美国每五例死亡中有一例与吸烟相关；显著增加心脏病、心脏病发作、中风、肺癌和慢性阻塞性肺病死亡风险
text_en: Smoking causes death. People who smoke typically die at an earlier age than non-smokers. In fact, one of every five deaths in the United States is linked to cigarette smoking. If you smoke your risk of major health problems increases dramatically, including heart disease, heart attack, stroke, lung cancer, and death from chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟与心血管疾病（一）：尼古丁刺激肾上腺释放肾上腺素导致血压升高；尼古丁和一氧化碳损伤动脉内壁，脂肪沉积形成斑块，导致动脉粥样硬化，严重减少血流
text_en: Smoking causes cardiovascular disease. When nicotine flows through your adrenal glands, it stimulates the release of epinephrine, a hormone that raises your blood pressure. In addition, nicotine and carbon monoxide can damage the lining of the inner walls in your arteries. Fatty deposits, called plaque, can build up at these injury sites and become large enough to narrow the arteries and severely reduce blood flow, resulting in a condition called atherosclerosis.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟与心血管疾病（二）：冠状动脉疾病中动脉粥样硬化使心脏供氧减少，增加心脏病发作风险；吸烟促进血小板聚集形成血栓；引发外周血管疾病阻塞四肢大动脉；可导致腹主动脉瘤
text_en: In coronary artery disease, atherosclerosis narrows the arteries that supply the heart, which reduces the supply of oxygen to your heart muscle, increasing your risk for a heart attack. Smoking also raises your risk for blood clots, because it causes platelets in your blood to clump together. Smoking increases your risk for peripheral vascular disease, in which atherosclerotic plaques block the large arteries in your arms and legs. Smoking can also cause an abdominal aortic aneurysm, which is a swelling or weakening of your aorta where it runs through your abdomen.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 肺部结构与正常呼吸过程：肺部的两个主要结构——支气管和肺泡；空气经气管进入支气管再到肺泡，完成氧气和二氧化碳的气体交换；纤毛负责清除有害物质
text_en: Smoking damages two main parts of your lungs. Your airways also called bronchial tubes, and small air sacs called alveoli. With each breath, air travels down your windpipe, called the trachea, and enters your lungs through your bronchial tubes. Air then moves in two thousands of tiny alveoli, where oxygen from the air moves into your blood stream, and the waste product carbon dioxide moves out of your blood stream. Tiny hair-like projections, called cilia, line your bronchial tubes and sweep harmful substances out of your lungs.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟对气道的损害：烟雾刺激支气管内壁导致肿胀和黏液增多；减缓纤毛运动使烟雾和黏液滞留肺中；睡眠时纤毛部分恢复，晨起咳嗽排出污染物（吸烟者咳嗽）；长期发展为慢性支气管炎
text_en: Cigarette smoke irritates the lining of your bronchial tubes, causing them to swell and make Cigarette smoke also slows the movement of your cilia, causing some of the smoke and mucus to stay in your lungs. While you're sleeping, some of the cilia recover and start pushing more pollutants and mucus out of your lungs. When you wake up, your body attempts to expel this material by coughing repeatedly, a condition known as smokers cough. Over time, chronic bronchitis develops as your cilia stop working, your airways become clogged with scars and mucus, and breathing becomes difficult. Your lungs are now more vulnerable to further disease.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 肺泡损伤与慢性阻塞性肺病（COPD）：烟雾损伤肺泡，阻碍气体交换；长期导致肺气肿，患者呼吸困难需佩戴氧气管；慢性支气管炎和肺气肿统称为COPD，不可逆转
text_en: Cigarette smoke also damages your LVLI, making it harder for oxygen and carbon dioxide to exchange with your blood. Over time, so little oxygen can reach your blood that you may develop emphysema, a condition in which you must gasp for every breath and wear an oxygen tube under your nose in order to breathe. Chronic bronchitis and emphysema are collectively called chronic obstructive pulmonary disease COPD is a gradual loss of the ability to breathe for which there is no cure.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟致癌机制：香烟含至少40种致癌物（氰化物、甲醛、苯、氨）；有毒化学物质损伤健康细胞的DNA，导致受损细胞失控增殖并可能扩散；肺癌是全球最常见癌症
text_en: Cigarette smoke contains at least 40 cancer-causing substances called carcinogens, including cyanide, formaldehyde, benzene, and ammonia. In your body, healthy cells grow, make new cells, then die. Genetic material inside each cell, called DNA, directs this process. If you smoke toxic chemicals, can damage the DNA in your healthy cells. As a result, your damaged cells create new unhealthy cells, which grow out of control and may spread to other parts of your body. The most common cancer in the world is lung cancer, with over a million new cases diagnosed every year.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟可导致的其他部位癌症：血液和骨髓、口腔、喉部、咽喉、食道、胃、胰腺、肾脏、膀胱、子宫和宫颈的癌症
text_en: Harmful chemicals in cigarettes can cause cancer in other parts of your body, such as, in the blood and bone marrow, mouth, larynx, throat, esophagus, stomach, pancreas, kidney, bladder, uterus, and cervix.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟对生育和妊娠的影响：导致男女不育；孕期吸烟增加低出生体重、流产、早产、死产、婴儿死亡及婴儿猝死综合征风险；哺乳期尼古丁通过母乳传递给婴儿，引起烦躁、心跳加速、呕吐等症状
text_en: Cigarette can cause infertility in both men and women. If a woman is pregnant and smokes during pregnancy, she exposes her baby to the cigarette's poisonous chemicals, causing a greater risk of low birth weight, miscarriage, pre-term delivery, still birth, infant death, and sudden infant death syndrome. Hitting is also dangerous if a mother is breastfeeding. Nicotine passes to the baby through breast milk, and can cause restlessness, rapid heartbeat, vomiting, interrupted sleep, or diarrhea.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟的其他健康影响：女性骨密度降低和髋部骨折风险增加、牙龈疾病导致牙齿脱落、免疫系统功能障碍、伤口愈合延迟、男性性功能障碍
text_en: Other health effects of smoking include low bone density and increased risk for hip fracture among women, gum disease often leading to tooth loss and surgery, immune system dysfunction, delayed wound healing, and sexual impotence in men.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心脏基本结构与正常血液循环：介绍心脏四个腔室（右心房、右心室、左心房、左心室），以及缺氧血从体循环回流经右心至肺部获取氧气，再经左心泵出至全身的正常循环过程。
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your body needs. The pumping sections of the heart are the right atrium, right ventricle, left atrium, and left ventricle. In your heart, oxygen poor blood flows from your body through large veins into your right atrium. Next, your blood moves into your right ventricle, which contracts, sending blood out of the heart to pick up oxygen from your lungs. Oxygen rich blood moves out of your lungs into your left atrium, then moves into your left ventricle. Finally, your left ventricle contracts, sending oxygen rich blood out of your heart to your

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的定义与基本病理：心脏丧失足够泵血能力，可能因心室壁薄弱无力（收缩功能障碍）或心室壁僵硬增厚导致充盈不足（舒张功能障碍）。
text_en: If you have heart failure, your heart has lost the ability to pump enough blood to meet your body's needs. You may have weak or damaged ventricular walls that are not able to push enough blood out of your heart. You may also have stiff and thickened ventricular walls that do not allow your heart to fill with enough blood.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 左心衰竭的病理机制：左心室泵血不足导致疲劳和呼吸困难；左心衰竭使肺静脉压力升高，液体渗入肺组织，造成肺水肿和呼吸困难。
text_en: If you have left-sided heart failure, your left ventricle does not deliver enough oxygen-rich blood to your body, making you feel tired and out of breath. Your failing left ventricle also increases the blood pressure in the blood vessels between your lungs and left ventricle. This increased pressure forces fluid out of your blood and into your lung tissues, which makes it difficult for you to breathe.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 右心衰竭的病理机制与心衰进展：右心室收缩力不足无法将血液泵入肺部，导致静脉血液淤积和全身组织水肿；长期心衰导致心室扩大、泵血量进一步减少的恶性循环。
text_en: If you have right-sided heart failure, your right ventricle is unable to contract with enough force to push blood to your lungs. The result is a build-up of blood in your veins, which causes a build-up of fluid called edema throughout the tissues in your body. Over time, heart failure on either side of your heart results in weakened and large ventricles that deliver less blood to your body.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 神经内分泌代偿机制及其危害：神经系统释放应激激素加快心率和增强收缩力以代偿泵血不足，但长期应激激素反而损伤心肌细胞，加重心衰。
text_en: To make up for the decreased amount of blood, your nervous system releases stress hormones that increase the speed and force of your heart beat. Unfortunately, the continued release of these stress hormones makes your heart failure worse because they damage the muscle cells in your ventricles.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的病因：最常见病因为冠状动脉疾病；其他病因包括高血压、糖尿病、心脏瓣膜病变、心肌病变、心律失常、先天性心脏缺陷、中毒或药物滥用、肺部疾病及睡眠呼吸暂停。
text_en: The most common cause of heart failure is coronary artery disease. Other causes that damage your heart and lead to heart failure include high blood pressure, diabetes, diseased infected or damaged heart valves, diseased infected or damaged heart muscle, irregular heartbeats called arrhythmias, heart defects, poisons or substance abuse, lung diseases, and breathing problems during sleep called sleep apnea.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心衰的药物治疗：利尿剂通过增加排尿减轻水肿；ACE抑制剂扩张血管、降低血压、减轻心脏泵血负担；β受体阻滞剂阻断应激激素对心脏的损害作用，保护心肌。
text_en: Treatment for heart failure includes medications, lifestyle changes, or in extreme heart failure, heart beat assisting devices or surgery. Medications reduce the swelling in your body by increasing the amount of urine produced by your kidneys. Ace inhibitors are medications that allow your blood vessels to expand. This helps decrease your blood pressure and prevents further damage to your heart by making it easier for your heart to pump blood. Beta blockers are medications that block the effects of stress hormones on your heart. Although beta blockers slow down your heart beat, they are mainly used to protect your heart muscle from the long-term damage caused by stress hormones.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心衰的生活方式调整：规律锻炼、保持健康体重、戒烟、限制盐和酒精摄入、采用有益心脏健康的饮食。
text_en: You may need to make some lifestyle changes, including exercising on a regular basis, maintaining a healthy weight, quitting smoking, limiting salt and alcohol, and eating a heart-healthy diet.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心衰的手术治疗：冠状动脉搭桥术改善心肌血供、心脏瓣膜修复术、左心室重建术切除受损心肌；极重度心衰可植入心室辅助装置或进行心脏移植。
text_en: Surgery for advanced heart failure include coronary artery bypass surgery to improve blood flow to your heart muscle, heart valve reconstruction surgery, to improve blood flow through your heart, and left ventricle reconstruction surgery to remove damaged heart muscle. Surgeries for extreme heart failure include insertion of a device to help your heart pump blood, and heart transplant.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
summary_zh: 心脏四腔结构：上方为左右心房，下方为左右心室，按稳定节律收缩形成心跳。正常心跳时血液从组织和肺流入心房再进入心室。房间隔和室间隔防止左右两侧血液混合。
text_en: Your heart has four chambers. Two upper chambers, called the left and right atria, and two lower chambers, called the left and right ventricles, contract in a steady rhythm known as your heart beat. During a normal heart beat, blood from your tissues and lungs flows into your atria, then into your ventricles. Inside your heart, called the inter atrial and interventricular septa, help keep the blood on left and right sides from mixing.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
summary_zh: 房室瓣膜：三尖瓣和二尖瓣如同门一样位于心房与心室之间，防止血液回流。腱索（corda tendonae）在心室强力收缩时固定瓣膜位置。
text_en: Two valves sit like doors between your atria and ventricles to prevent blood from flowing backward into your atria. The tricuspid valve opens into your right ventricle, and the mitral valve opens into your left ventricle. Strong, thin tissues, called corda tendonae, hold your valves in place during the forceful contractions of your ventricles.
